clingy.definition: Wanting to stay very close to someone, not wanting to be apart from them.
clingy.example: The toddler was clingy and wouldn't let go of his mother.

stage.definition: A period or step in a process of growth or change.
stage.example: Every child goes through different stages of development.

bond.definition: A strong feeling of closeness and connection between people.
bond.example: The mother and baby formed a strong bond early on.

capacity.definition: The ability to do or experience something.
capacity.example: Children have a great capacity for learning new things.

prefer.definition: To like one thing more than another.
prefer.example: Most babies prefer to be held by their parents.

immediate.definition: Closest or most direct, without anything in between.
immediate.example: Her immediate family includes her parents and brother.

carers.definition: People who take care of someone, especially children or sick people.
carers.example: The nursery carers looked after the children during the day.

secure.definition: Feeling safe and free from worry.
secure.example: A secure child feels confident to explore the world.

separate.definition: To move apart from someone or something.
separate.example: Children learn to separate from their parents gradually.

clinging.definition: Holding on tightly and not wanting to let go.
clinging.example: The baby was clinging to her father's shirt.

longing.definition: A strong feeling of wanting something or someone very much.
longing.example: She felt a longing to see her family again.

overwhelming.definition: So strong or so much that it is hard to deal with.
overwhelming.example: The amount of homework felt overwhelming to the student.

attachment.definition: A strong emotional connection to someone.
attachment.example: Babies form attachments to their main caregivers.

reassurance.definition: Words or actions that make someone feel less worried.
reassurance.example: The child needed reassurance that everything would be okay.

keen.definition: Very interested in or eager to do something.
keen.example: He was keen to try the new game.

granted.definition: In 'take for granted': to not appreciate something because you are used to it.
granted.example: We often take our health for granted.

playdates.definition: Planned times for children to play together.
playdates.example: She arranged playdates with her daughter's friends.

sleepovers.definition: When a child sleeps at a friend's house for the night.
sleepovers.example: The kids loved having sleepovers on weekends.

fostering.definition: Encouraging something to grow or develop.
fostering.example: Reading to children helps in fostering a love of books.

independent.definition: Able to do things by yourself without needing help from others.
independent.example: As children grow, they become more independent.

spirit.definition: The quality of being brave, energetic, or determined.

paradoxically.definition: In a way that seems opposite to what you expect but is actually true.
paradoxically.example: Paradoxically, the harder you try to sleep, the harder it becomes.

sensitive.definition: Easily affected by things; feeling things strongly.
sensitive.example: Some children are more sensitive to loud noises.

pace.definition: The speed at which something happens or develops.
pace.example: Every child learns at their own pace.

milestone.definition: An important event or achievement in development.
milestone.example: Walking is a major milestone for a baby.

worthy.example: Every person is worthy of love and care.

relational.definition: Connected to the relationships between people.

invest.definition: To put time, effort, or money into something to get a good result later.
invest.example: Parents invest a lot of energy in their children's early years.

positive.example: Positive attention helps children grow with confidence.

rush.definition: To try to do something too quickly; to hurry.
rush.example: Don't rush your child through their learning.

ignore.definition: To not pay attention to something or someone on purpose.
ignore.example: If you ignore a child's needs, they may feel unloved.

stuck.definition: Unable to move forward or make progress.
stuck.example: The child got stuck on the same problem for a long time.

development.definition: The process of growing and changing over time.
development.example: Early childhood is a critical time for brain development.
